us-politics.headline: King Charles Visits Trump, Warns on Executive Power
us-politics.body:
British monarch King Charles III met with President Trump in Washington, delivering a nuanced message about democratic checks and balances. During his state visit, Charles urged caution about unchecked executive authority - a rare diplomatic intervention that sparked mixed reactions across the political spectrum.

The royal visit included a state dinner and congressional address, with Charles reportedly discussing international issues like Iran's nuclear program. Republicans celebrated the visit, while some Democrats staged protests under the 'No Kings' banner, highlighting the complex political dynamics of the royal engagement.

sources.title: Trump welcomes King Charles III and Queen Camilla for state dinner
Trump says King Charles “agrees” Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon
King Charles urges checks on executive power as Trump hosts royal visit
Republicans swipe at Democrats applauding King Charles after ‘No Kings’ protests
5 takeaways as Britain’s King Charles meets Trump and addresses Congress

us-politics.headline: House Advances Spy Powers and Farm Bill Amid Debate
us-politics.body:
House Republicans are preparing critical votes on surveillance powers and agricultural policy, with internal party divisions emerging. The FISA 702 spy powers bill faces potential GOP opposition, reflecting ongoing tensions about government surveillance mechanisms.

Simultaneously, lawmakers are working on an ethanol compromise tied to the Farm Bill. The legislative maneuvering reveals the delicate balancing act between different Republican factions and their policy priorities.

sources.title: House panel advances three major bills after marathon hearing, but fate on floor uncertain
House Republicans tee up vote on spy powers as some in GOP vow to oppose bill
House tees up vote on ethanol compromise, which would attach to Farm Bill

us-politics.headline: Comey Indicted Again in Escalating Political Confrontation
us-politics.body:
Former FBI Director James Comey faces a new federal indictment related to a 2025 social media post allegedly threatening President Trump. The legal action underscores the ongoing tension between Trump and intelligence community figures who have been critical of his administration.

Comey responded by stating this prosecution does not represent American democratic values. The indictment represents another chapter in the prolonged political and legal conflicts surrounding Trump's presidency and its aftermath.

sources.title: Comey responds to indictment: ‘This is not who we are as a country’
James Comey indicted over 2025 social media post allegedly threatening Trump

au-politics.headline: Inflation Rises, RBA Faces Tough Interest Rate Decision
au-politics.body: Australia's monthly inflation jumped to 4.6% in March, driven by higher fuel prices from the ongoing Iran conflict. The surge puts pressure on the Reserve Bank of Australia to reconsider its interest rate strategy. Economists suggest the data may prompt a pause in rate hikes - a potential shift from recent monetary policy.

sources.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
Inflation jumps to 4.6% in Australia as Iran war fuel shock begins to bite
Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent as higher fuel prices bite

au-politics.headline: Political Tensions Simmer: Hanson, Rinehart, and Party Dynamics
au-politics.body: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson received a private plane from mining billionaire Gina Rinehart, sparking renewed debate about political donations and influence. Former Liberal leader Malcolm Turnbull warned that the party risks becoming a recruiting agent for extremist groups by echoing One Nation's policy approaches. The gift highlights ongoing tensions within Australia's conservative political landscape.

sources.title: Australia's richest woman gifts plane to Pauline Hanson
Pauline Hanson boasts about ‘sexy’ new private plane gifted by Gina Rinehart and $2m in donations
By echoing the hateful policies of One Nation, the Liberal party risks becoming a recruiting agent for extremist groups | Malcolm Turnbull

au-politics.headline: AI Deepfakes Emerge as New Challenge in Australian Schools
au-politics.body: Schools across Australia are grappling with the rising threat of AI-generated deepfake content. Educators and policymakers are seeking effective strategies to address the technological challenge that can potentially harm student relationships and school environments. The issue underscores the rapid technological changes confronting educational institutions in the digital age.

sources.title: AI deepfakes are in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?

world.headline: US-Iran Conflict Escalates - Regional Tensions Spike
world.body:
The ongoing US war against Iran has triggered cascading geopolitical consequences across the Middle East. UN aid chiefs warn that Somalia faces worsening humanitarian conditions due to the conflict, while the UAE has dramatically quit OPEC - signaling the war's broader economic impact.

Oil markets are experiencing significant volatility. Regional powers are recalibrating alliances, with the conflict threatening to destabilize multiple countries beyond the immediate theater of war.

sources.title: UN aid chief says situation in Somalia worsened by US war on Iran
UAE quits OPEC as oil cartel takes blow during war on Iran
Iran war live: Trump says Tehran wants end to blockade; Israel kills medics
As US-Iran talks remain ‘stalled’, experts warn of ‘long-term disruptions’

world.headline: Israel Continues Gaza Assault - Medics Killed in Strike
world.body:
Lebanese Prime Minister condemned Israeli military actions as war crimes after an attack killed three rescue workers in Gaza. Graphic evidence suggests extensive destruction - with one Israeli soldier documenting Beit Hanoon as completely flattened.

The ongoing assault continues to draw international condemnation, with mounting concerns about civilian casualties and potential escalation of regional tensions.

sources.title: Lebanon’s PM slams Israel’s ‘war crimes’ as attack kills 3 rescue workers
Israeli soldier shows Gaza’s Beit Hanoon completely flattened
Iran war live: Trump says Tehran wants end to blockade; Israel kills medics

world.headline: Trump Era Continues - Passports and Political Maneuvers
world.body:
The US will release limited-edition passports featuring former President Trump's image for the country's 250th anniversary. Meanwhile, Trump remains politically active - criticizing Germany's leadership over Iran war commentary and welcoming British royals.

Legal and political developments continue to swirl around Trump, with a US appeals court rejecting one of his immigration detention policies and the Justice Department again indicting former FBI director James Comey.

sources.title: Trump to feature on limited-edition US passports for 250th anniversary
US appeals court rejects Trump’s immigration detention policy
Comey charged with threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
Former FBI director Comey again indicted by US Justice Department
Trump hails US-UK bond as he welcomes British royals
Trump scolds Germany’s Merz for criticism of Iran war

ai.headline: Musk Takes OpenAI to Court - Friendship and AI's Future at Stake
ai.body:
Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI has erupted into a deeply personal legal battle. Musk testified that he founded the organization to prevent a potential Terminator-like AI apocalypse - a mission he claims the company has abandoned by prioritizing commercial interests over safety.

The trial has exposed the fractured relationship between Musk and OpenAI leadership, particularly Sam Altman. Observers noted Musk appeared more emotional than strategic, relitigating an old friendship while attempting to paint OpenAI's current direction as a betrayal of its original mission.

sources.title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
Elon Musk appeared more petty than prepared
Elon Musk Testifies That He Started OpenAI to Prevent a ‘Terminator Outcome’
Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
Elon Musk takes the stand in high-profile trial against OpenAI

ai.headline: AI Giants Expand Cloud Services and Creative Capabilities
ai.body:
Major tech companies are rapidly expanding AI integration across cloud and creative platforms. Amazon has already begun offering new OpenAI products through AWS, signaling a strategic partnership that could reshape enterprise AI deployment.

Anthropic is pushing creative boundaries with Claude, highlighting new capabilities for artistic and professional work. Meanwhile, the competitive landscape continues to shift - with companies like Google expanding military AI contracts and Meta facing potential workforce reductions in AI training operations.

sources.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
OpenAI models coming to Amazon Bedrock: Interview with OpenAI and AWS CEOs
Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
Claude for Creative Work
‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal

btc.headline: Bitcoin Surges to $77,000 as Institutional Interest Grows
btc.body:
Bitcoin rocketed past $77,000 on Wednesday, driven by strategic institutional purchases and growing market confidence. Bitwise's Chief Investment Officer suggested the current rally could sustain momentum, with institutional money slowly but steadily entering the market.

The price surge comes amid complex geopolitical tensions and ahead of a critical Federal Reserve decision. Veteran crypto analyst Adam Back cautioned that institutional adoption moves more deliberately than many expect - a nuanced perspective tempering market excitement.

sources.title: Bitcoin rises to $77,000 ahead of Fed decision as Trump preps for lengthy Hormuz block
Bitcoin’s recent rally is largely fueled by Strategy purchases: Bitwise's Hougan
Institutional money is coming for bitcoin, but Adam Back says it moves slower than you think
Paul Tudor Jones calls bitcoin the 'best inflation hedge,' warns of overvalued stocks
Strategy-fueled bitcoin rally could last ‘for some time to come,’ Bitwise CIO says

btc.headline: Bitcoin Gains Ground in Traditional Finance Landscape
btc.body:
Major financial voices are increasingly positioning Bitcoin as a transformative asset class. Paul Tudor Jones labeled Bitcoin the best inflation hedge while warning about overvalued stock markets - a sentiment echoing broader institutional shifts.

The Czech Central Bank's chief made waves by backing Bitcoin as a potential 1% reserve asset, signaling growing mainstream acceptance. Block's recent announcement that 800,000 merchants now accept Bitcoin further underscores the cryptocurrency's expanding real-world utility.

sources.title: Bitcoin is Reshaping Traditional Finance, Industry Leaders Say
Czech Central Bank Chief Backs Bitcoin as 1% Reserve Slice in ‘Conservative but Innovative’ Strategy
Paul Tudor Jones calls bitcoin the 'best inflation hedge,' warns of overvalued stocks
Block (XYZ) Touts Bitcoin as ‘Everyday Money’ With 800,000 Merchants Now Accepting It
